# Sub PEQ filters
# miniDSP-style peaking filters plus normalized biquad coefficients
# Biquad convention: y[n] = b0*x[n] + b1*x[n-1] + b2*x[n-2] - a1*y[n-1] - a2*y[n-2]

Filter 1: ON PK Fc 179.810 Hz Gain +3.000 dB Q 6.6667
  biquad b0=1.00030613559 b1=-1.99837744782 b2=0.9982097057 a1=-1.99837744782 a2=0.998515841294
Filter 2: ON PK Fc 106.934 Hz Gain +3.000 dB Q 3.3186
  biquad b0=1.00036569167 b1=-1.99817817144 b2=0.997861419181 a1=-1.99817817144 a2=0.99822711085
Filter 3: ON PK Fc 57.861 Hz Gain -5.244 dB Q 4.2294
  biquad b0=0.999725773444 b1=-1.99877550596 b2=0.999064065293 a1=-1.99877550596 a2=0.998789838737
Filter 4: ON PK Fc 15.015 Hz Gain +3.000 dB Q 6.6667
  biquad b0=1.00002558115 b1=-1.9998750158 b2=0.999850400292 a1=-1.9998750158 a2=0.999875981447
Filter 5: ON PK Fc 46.875 Hz Gain +2.993 dB Q 2.1408
  biquad b0=1.00024801268 b1=-1.99878503458 b2=0.998546428612 a1=-1.99878503458 a2=0.998794441292
